metadata.description: Curated marketplace of Claude Code plugins for developer frameworks, productivity, accessibility, SEO, and DevOps. Each plugin is built for a specific job — code review, project planning, accessibility audits, secret management, Coolify ops, Supabase audits, and so on — with explicit honesty about what each does and doesn't do. Pair them where it makes sense; use them standalone where it doesn't. Version history lives in CHANGELOG.md.

plugins.name: rad-code-review
plugins.description: 3-role adversarial code review with diff-aware scoping and AI slop detection — blame-aware diff filtering, framework-specific IDOR heuristics, 14 AI slop patterns, performance profiling, WCAG 2.2 dynamic ARIA state detection, interactive findings management with accept/filter/fix workflow, and severity-ranked findings with optional fix application.

plugins.name: rad-chrome-extension
plugins.description: Comprehensive MV3 Chrome extension development standards — WXT framework architecture, MV3 security, permission minimization, typed messaging, storage patterns, service worker lifecycle, React UI, testing, and Chrome Web Store troubleshooting.

plugins.name: rad-a11y
plugins.description: Digital accessibility toolkit for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance — semantic HTML, ARIA authoring practices, keyboard navigation, focus management, color contrast, motion/animation, form accessibility, SVG accessibility, and automated testing with axe-core.

plugins.name: rad-supabase
plugins.description: End-to-end Supabase development and operations — projects, database, migrations, edge functions, auth, storage, realtime, branching, security, and troubleshooting. Covers both Supabase CLI and Supabase MCP tool usage.

plugins.name: rad-coolify-orchestrator
plugins.description: End-to-end Coolify self-hosted PaaS management — deployments, databases, security, CI/CD, troubleshooting, observability, and multi-server infrastructure. Coolify v4 self-hosted.

plugins.name: rad-1password
plugins.description: End-to-end 1Password CLI (op) coverage — secret references (op://...), `op inject`/`op run`/`op read` for keeping secrets out of code and shell history, item CRUD with assignment statements and JSON templates, vault and user provisioning with granular permissions, SSH key generation and use, third-party CLI auth via `op plugin run`, and service-account / Connect-server patterns for CI/CD and headless servers. 1 router skill, 6 topical sub-skills, 4 slash commands. Cross-checked against op v2.34.0 --help.

plugins.name: rad-brainstormer
plugins.description: Collaborative ideation using proven methodologies (SCAMPER, Six Hats, Reverse Brainstorming, 5 Whys, HMW), domain research, structured evaluation, and design sprints.

plugins.name: rad-seo-optimizer
plugins.description: Comprehensive SEO auditing, AEO/GEO optimization, competitor intelligence, keyword discovery, link building, content strategy, schema markup, and E-E-A-T optimization.

plugins.name: rad-context-prompter
plugins.description: Production-grade prompt engineering toolkit — systematic tool-specific routing across 30+ AI platforms, anti-pattern diagnostics, technique selection matrix, prompt decompilation, autonomous debugging, context engineering for agentic systems, and reusable templates.

plugins.name: rad-para-second-brain
plugins.description: Active PARA system management — weekly review automation, folder structure auditing, Progressive Summarization, Intermediate Packet assembly, Hemingway Bridge session handoffs, and 12 Favorite Problems workshop.

plugins.name: rad-session
plugins.description:
Make AI coding sessions remember each other — across machines, across agents (Claude + Codex), across days. Three commands manage the lifecycle: `/startup` orients you in under five seconds, `/wrapup` writes status from evidence (git diff, test output, plan state) rather than chat memory, and `/add-resource` registers new tools as you discover them.

Built around a canonical doc set — operating manual (CLAUDE.md and/or AGENTS.md per agent scope), `docs/status.md`, `docs/planning/current.md`, `docs/decisions/` — aligned with published OpenAI/Anthropic project-structure research. Auto-bootstraps on first run, auto-commits to keep PC ↔ GitHub ↔ laptop in sync, and refuses to overwrite user-authored work even under autonomous permission modes. Pairs with rad-planner; works standalone.

plugins.name: rad-gws-core
plugins.description: Google Workspace core integration — 14 essential skills covering Gmail (send, read, reply, triage), Docs (write), Sheets (read, append), Slides, Drive, and Calendar. Requires the gws CLI binary.

plugins.name: rad-planner
plugins.description:
Plan a project before you write code — or replan after a pivot, deepen an existing plan, or check for drift between plan and reality. rad-planner walks you through a five-phase planning conversation (Constitution → Scope → Sequence → Quality Gates → Doc-Set Recommendation), then writes the approved doc set as the plan's first executed milestone. The plan is the deliverable; the docs follow it.

Four entry points cover the lifecycle: full plan (greenfield), improvement (deepen what's there), drift (compare reality to plan), and pivot (substantial re-plan). A read-only `--assessment` mode answers "where does this project stand?" without writing anything. Two project-story skills (`/project-story`, `/refresh-story`) turn the canonical doc set into plain-English narrative for non-developers, collaborators, or your future self. Four mechanical validators (plan-lint, status, doc-redundancy, doc-contradiction) back the workflow. Pairs with rad-session for the full lifecycle; works standalone.

plugins.name: rad-explain
plugins.description:
Explain a project to humans, honestly — without overpromising or inventing features that aren't in the repo. Five skills generate audience-targeted communications from your project's actual artifacts: `narrate-project` (plain-English description, audience-adaptive), `elevator-pitch` (~150 words / ~30 seconds spoken), `draft-pitch` (one-pager for funding, grants, partnerships), `explain-document` (interpret a specific repo file — "what does this commit me to?", "where could this go wrong?"), and `ground-readme` (generate or audit a README so it stays grounded in source).

Two pure-stdlib Python validators check every output: `check-grounding.py` traces each substantive claim back to repo source, and `check-overpromise.py` flags superlatives, marketing fluff, and production-readiness assertions without evidence. Works on any repo — no rad-planner dependency. Reads `docs/` if present, falls back to README + project manifest + source structure when absent. Pairs with `/rad-planner:project-story` for canonical-doc projects. The plugin's own README passes its own validators.
